member.name: Paul Holmes

addresses: The Hill, Upper Northam Close, Hedge End, SO30 4BB

member.synopsis: Paul Holmes is the Conservative MP for Hamble Valley , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the roles of Opposition Whip (Commons), and Shadow Parliamentary Under Secretary (Housing, Communities and Local Government).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Candidates: Equality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, further to the answer of 15 June 2026, to Question HL475, on Candidates: Equality, whether data is collected from political parties on the regulatory cost of such a provision; what elections will it apply to;
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon member to the answer given to PQ HL475. Office for Equality and Opportunity officials are currently exploring when and how to commence the provision under Section 106.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Members and Political Parties: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: All donations with a value above £500 must be checked for permissibility in accordance with the requirements set out in the Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act (PPERA). The Know Your Donor regime will introduce new risk-based due diligence ch...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Political Parties: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, pursuant to the answer of 17 June 2026, to Question 8263, on Political Parties: Finance, on what date were political parties informed that the retrospective ban from 25 March 202
writtenQuestions.answerText: Political parties were informed of the moratorium on crypto asset donations of any value, including those thresholds, in the Secretary of State’s announcement on 25 March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Costs

writtenQuestions.answerText: The estimated costs of parliamentary by-elections, including the Makerfield parliamentary by-election, are set out in the relevant schedule of The Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officers’ Charges) Order 2024. Local elections, including the costs of ma...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Air Conditioning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether standalone air conditioning units have permitted development rights in dwellings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no permitted development rights for standalone air conditioning units. However, in certain circumstances, planning permission is not required where it would not materially affect the external appearance of the building. Part P of the Building Re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no permitted development rights for standalone air conditioning units. However, in certain circumstances, planning permission is not required where it would not materially affect the external appearance of the building. Part P of the Building Re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Regeneration: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to his Department's press release entitled Pubs, shops and local assets protected with communities in charge, of 16 June 2026, what is the allocation of funding in
writtenQuestions.answerText: The newly announced Pride in Place Community Right to Buy Fund, backed by £61 million, will empower local people to take control of valued local assets and is additional to our wider investment in communities including the £5.8 billion Pride in Place Prog...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Anti-Muslim Hostility
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what progress his Department has made on the appointment of the Special Representative for Anti-Muslim Hostility; whether it will be a regulated public appointment, a special adv
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Terms of Reference and appointment process for the Special Representative on anti-Muslim hostility will be published in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Trade Unions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, With reference to Deposited Paper, DEP2026-0371, committed on 19 May 2026, on Civil Service facility time framework and guidance, and with reference to the DCLG guidance, Taxpaye
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 2013 DCLG guidance ‘Taxpayer funding of trade unions’ has been superseded by the Local Government Transparency Code 2015 and the Employment Rights Act 2025. The Transparency Code requires local authorities to annually publish certain information regar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Interreg Europe
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 9 June 2026 to Question HL478, on Interreg Europe, whether HM Government intends to discuss joining or contributing to EU Interreg in relation to Great Britain.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the value of the UK associating to EU programmes. Each EU programme is different and association to these programmes are made on a case by case basis, in line with the national interest.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, pursuant to the Answer of 8 June 2026 to Question 5349 on Sustainable Communities Act 2007, when was the last (a) proposal submitted to the Secretary of State and (b) invitation
writtenQuestions.answerText: The last proposal submitted under the Sustainable Communities Act 2007 was in November 2019. The government issued the second invitation to local authorities to submit proposals under the Act in 2010, and that invitation remains open.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Elections

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 9592 on 23 June 2026. The government agreed to meet the claimant’s reasonable legal costs, in line with standard practice where proceedings are settled. Those costs have now been agreed and paid ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Leukaemia: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce avoidable delays in the diagnosis of leukaemia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that the National Health Service diagnoses cancer earlier and treats it faster so that more patients, including those with leukaemia, survive.The National Disease Registration Service collects information on how man...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Pilot Schemes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he plans to use local election pilots under Section 10 of the Representation of the People Act 2000 to amend the (a) franchise and (b) registration criteria.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government has no plans to use powers under Section 10 of the Representation of the People Act 2000 to amend electoral franchise or registration criteria.The narrow powers under Section 10 of the R...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Identity Cards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the answer of 11 March 2026 to Question 118513 on Identity Cards: Northern Ireland, whether the new Digital ID will list the date of birth of the individual.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Yes, the new national digital ID will include your date of birth.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Proof of Identity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the answer of 20 April 2026 to Question 124770 on Elections: Proof of Identity, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of that policy on levels of
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such assessment has been made. Allowing UK‑authorised bank cards to be used at polling stations in Great Britain is intended to enable a greater proportion of legitimate electors to meet the voter identification requirements more easily. It is unaccept...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Infant Mortality: Temporary Accommodation

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer given to UIN 3379 on 1 June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Infant Mortality: Temporary Accommodation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce the rate of stillbirth and neonatal deaths, where the mother has recently lived or is living in temporary accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving outcomes for women and babies from vulnerable and underserved groups, and through the Tackling Child Poverty Strategy has committed to ending the practice of discharging newborn babies into bed and breakfasts or ot...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether housing status is recorded by maternity services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Maternity services seek to understand women’s housing circumstances at their first booking appointment and later appointments, so that where concerns arise, they can be referred for housing support where needed. Housing status can be recorded locally, but...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pregnancy: Homelessness
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve access to maternal pathways with tailored support for pregnant people experiencing homelessness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to improving outcomes for women and babies from vulnerable and underserved groups, including those experiencing homelessness, to ensure access to safe, personalised, and equitable maternity care.That is why the Government has committed to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
